I personally found this museum deeply disturbing and disappointing. Many of the exhibits felt less educational and more exploitative, turning real human suffering and death into entertainment. I left feeling uncomfortable with the way tragedy is commercialized here. In my opinion, the experience felt disrespectful and lacking in dignity. I would not recommend it to anyone looking for a thoughtful or meaningful museum experience.

As a librarian and someone who leans toward the academic, I did enjoy my time there but became skeptical at a few of the artifacts, particularly the anthropodermic bibliopegy. Flesh covered books aren't flesh colored... they are tanned like any other leather (sorry to be morbid but this is the theme, I suppose). I was listening to the docents talking as well and I just feel like this place was just really unprofessional as a museum. The exhibits were mid. If the artifacts were as they claim, then pretty cool... I like to believe they were. The taxidermy itself was great.
Do it if youre into true crime! Knowledgeable staff. Gruesome photos and even letters from the killers themselves! I enjoyed the experience, not sure I would take my 14 year old son though 🤔
